text.title: Crash-Pipeline-as-Code Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn an "we have crash tracking" claim into "we have un-symbolicated stacks." Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Silent symbol-upload failure — CI step returns success but vendor never indexed the symbols.
failure-mode.detector: symbol-manifest entry exists with upload_status=200 BUT synthetic_verification_passed_at is null OR > 30 minutes stale
failure-mode.repair: Add the verify-symbol-upload step (r2). Treat verification failure as a release-blocking error. Re-run upload OR investigate vendor-side processing.

failure-mode.description: Expired or rotated auth token quietly disables uploads — CI passes (no auth required), but vendor returns 401.
failure-mode.detector: upload_status across last 3 releases includes 401/403 OR vendor dashboard shows 0 symbols indexed for recent releases
failure-mode.repair: Add token-expiry check to CI (vendor API call before upload). Rotate via secret manager with calendar alarm. Store rotation date in retention.yaml.

failure-mode.description: Console drift — admin added an alert rule in the console, not committed; rule disappears in a vendor migration.
failure-mode.detector: Daily drift-check: list vendor rules via API, compare to crash-pipeline/alert-rules/. Diff > 0.
failure-mode.repair: Export drifted rule to code OR delete from vendor. Add the drift-check job to CI (daily cron). Treat drift like infra drift — fix immediately.

failure-mode.description: PII leakage — crash payloads include user emails, tokens, request bodies, sent to vendor unredacted.
failure-mode.detector: Sample 5 events; grep payload for emails, tokens, common name lists. Or vendor's data-scrubbing dashboard shows 0% scrubbed.
failure-mode.repair: Set vendor-side data-scrubbing rules in retention.yaml. Wrap exception handlers to strip PII before sending. Test by emitting a synthetic event with PII and confirming it arrives scrubbed.

failure-mode.description: Dashboard "owned by the team" — actually owned by no one. Rots into stale metrics.
failure-mode.detector: Dashboard owner field empty or "team" AND last_modified > 6 months
failure-mode.repair: Assign a named owner per dashboard (rotation OK quarterly). Quarterly review ritual: each owner walks their dashboard and either updates or proposes removal.

failure-mode.description: AI-generated alert rule fires too aggressively (every minor exception page) and team mutes the channel.
failure-mode.detector: Alert channel has muted notifications OR alert rule fires > 50x/day with no actionable response
failure-mode.repair: Tune threshold and group-by (per release, per app version). Mute channels are a symptom of bad rules, not bad team discipline. Use a fresh-events / users-affected percentile rather than raw count.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: If LLM helps generate alert rules from incident postmortems, generated rules MUST be human-reviewed before merge. Auto-merged AI rules become noise generators.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Production-readiness review on each release: a human (release captain or on-call) confirms symbol-manifest verification passed, no drift on alert rules, and the primary dashboard is green. Sign-off in the release ticket.
